intent: A high-fidelity project management platform leveraging predictive AI to automate task dependency mapping and resource allocation. Unlike linear Kanban boards, it provides dynamic 'drift analysis' that alerts project leads when timeline slippage occurs based on individual velocity patterns, ultimately consolidating sprint planning, document repository, and cross-team communication into a unified workspace.

entities.name: VelocityProfile
entities.description: A persistent record tracking individual contributor throughput rates and historical task completion timeframes to calibrate predictive modeling.

entities.name: DependencyGraphNode
entities.description: Represents a discrete unit of work within a Directed Acyclic Graph, capturing hard and soft constraints between inter-departmental tasks.

entities.name: DriftVector
entities.description: A mathematical representation of the variance between planned project trajectory and real-time execution telemetry, used to trigger proactive stakeholder alerts.

entities.name: ResourceAllocationManifest
entities.description: A dynamic ledger that tracks human and capital resource availability, mapped against the temporal requirements of active DependencyGraphNodes.

entities.name: IntegrationWebhookBridge
entities.description: A middleware component that normalizes incoming event payloads from external tools like GitHub or Jira into the platform's internal domain event stream.

entities.name: WorkspaceContext
entities.description: An isolated multi-tenant container housing project state, document references, and cross-team communication threads.

entities.name: PermissionGuardrail
entities.description: An RBAC policy object that evaluates granular access rights against specific WorkspaceContext assets during API request processing.

architecture.description: The system architecture utilizes a reactive microservices pattern where the IntegrationWebhookBridge ingests external events, triggering updates to the DependencyGraphNode cluster. As task statuses change, the VelocityProfile service recalculates projected timelines, generating DriftVector artifacts that are evaluated against the ResourceAllocationManifest. When a critical variance is detected, a notification engine pushes alerts to the WorkspaceContext. Data is persisted in a normalized relational schema for consistency, while high-velocity telemetry data is processed asynchronously through an event bus to maintain sub-second latency for UI updates across distributed teams.

endpoints.summary: Ingest external event payloads for normalization
List all dependency graph nodes for a specific workspace
Retrieve throughput and historical profile for a task node
Retrieve variance vectors for a specific task node
Fetch the resource allocation manifest for a specific task
Define or update access policy guardrails for a workspace
validation_rules: policy_definition must be a valid JSON object

rules.description: Ensure acyclic integrity of the dependency graph during node updates.
rules.logic: Validate that the new edge does not complete a closed cycle before committing to the DependencyGraphNode cluster.

rules.description: Strict state transition validation for project tasks.
rules.logic: Prohibit transition to 'Completed' status if active dependencies in the DependencyGraphNode cluster remain in 'Pending' or 'In-Progress' states.

rules.description: Synchronize ResourceAllocationManifest with active task temporal requirements.
rules.logic: If a DependencyGraphNode duration exceeds the allocated buffer in the ResourceAllocationManifest, recalculate the DriftVector.

rules.description: Permission enforcement for cross-tenant data access.
rules.logic: Reject all cross-entity mutations if the PermissionGuardrail policy object does not match the requesting identity's claims against the specific WorkspaceContext.
Generate a high-priority alert notification only if the DriftVector variance exceeds the pre-defined 15% tolerance threshold relative to the ResourceAllocationManifest.
Discard any event payload from the IntegrationWebhookBridge that fails schema validation or lacks a valid mapping to an existing WorkspaceContext UID.
